channel.title: Ventilation on Budget Van Life Affiliate Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Ventilation on Budget Van Life Affiliate Guide

item.title: CRL T-Vent Windows for ProMaster Review
item.description:
CRL T-Vent Windows for ProMaster Review
#

I lived in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500 full-time for seven years, and windows were one of the upgrades that most improved daily quality of life. Better airflow, natural light, and less cave-like interior feel can change how long you can comfortably stay inside your van. This review covers CRL T-Vent windows from a practical full-time-use perspective, not a showroom perspective.

item.title: MaxxAir 4500K Fan Review: Budget-Friendly Ventilation for Van Life
item.description:
MaxxAir 4500K Fan Review: Budget-Friendly Ventilation for Van Life
#

I spent seven years living full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, and one lesson never changed: ventilation is foundational. It is easy to overspend on gadgets and underspend on airflow. The MaxxAir 4500K is often considered the value option in this category, so I tested it as a real daily-use solution and compared it with higher-end alternatives.

item.title: MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K Review: Best Fan for Van Life?
item.description:
MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K Review: Best Fan for Van Life?
#

After seven years full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, I consider roof ventilation non-negotiable. A quality fan affects sleep quality, condensation control, cooking comfort, and even how stuffy the van feels by afternoon. I tested multiple fan setups over time, and this review focuses on the MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K from a real full-time-use perspective.
